opportunities.benefits: Gift cards escalating from $10 to $599 total for negative stimulant drug tests (meth/cocaine).
opportunities.eligibility: Medi-Cal recipients in participating stimulant-use treatment in SF.
opportunities.process: Enroll through participating behavioral health provider. Complete drug tests at scheduled intervals.
opportunities.notes: Statewide program through CA DHCS. SF providers include SFAF, HealthRIGHT 360, GLIDE.

opportunities.benefits: Gift cards up to $330 for negative stimulant drug tests. HIV/Hep C testing. MAT access.
opportunities.eligibility: People who use stimulants in San Francisco. No insurance required.
opportunities.process: Walk in to Strut (470 Castro St) or 6th Street Center (117 6th St). Enroll in program.
checklist: Walk in to Strut or 6th St Center
opportunities.notes: No insurance or Medi-Cal required. Walk-in enrollment.

opportunities.benefits: Gift cards up to $599 for negative drug tests. Full addiction treatment continuum.
opportunities.eligibility: People seeking substance use treatment in SF.
opportunities.process: Call intake line or walk in. Enroll in CM program alongside treatment.
opportunities.notes: Part of statewide Recovery Incentives Program.

opportunities.benefits: Free buprenorphine, contingency management (gift cards), counseling, no appointment needed.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone with opioid or stimulant use disorder. No insurance required.
opportunities.process: Walk in during open hours. First-come first-served.
opportunities.notes: No insurance, no appointment, no cost. Highly accessible.

opportunities.benefits: Free Hep C testing, treatment, curative medication. Free wellness navigation.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone at risk for or living with Hep C in SF.
opportunities.process: Walk in to 6th Street Center (117 6th St) or Strut (470 Castro St). No appointment.
checklist: Walk in to 6th St Center or Strut
opportunities.notes: Hep C is curable with 8-12 weeks of medication. All free.

opportunities.benefits: Free Hep C testing, peer navigation to treatment, linkage to care.
opportunities.eligibility: San Franciscans living with or at risk for hepatitis C.
opportunities.process: Visit website to find a testing site, or contact directly.
opportunities.notes: Citywide initiative to eliminate Hep C.

opportunities.benefits: Free HIV testing, PrEP/PEP, STI screening, counseling. All services free.
opportunities.eligibility: Gay, bi, trans men and all people seeking sexual health services. No requirements.
opportunities.process: Walk in or make appointment online.
opportunities.notes: All sexual health services free. Castro location.

opportunities.benefits: Free HIV/Hep C/STI testing, syringe access, MAT, harm reduction services.
opportunities.eligibility: People who use drugs, people experiencing homelessness, SOMA residents.
opportunities.process: Walk in. No appointment needed.
opportunities.notes: Low-barrier harm reduction hub in SOMA. All services free.

opportunities.benefits: Free 12-week peer support training + 3 SFSU Continuing Ed units. Certificate.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to public. Priority for people with lived experience.
opportunities.process: Contact RAMS Peer Wellness Center to enroll in next cohort.
opportunities.notes: Free SFSU Continuing Ed units included. Leads to paid peer support positions ($18-25/hr).

opportunities.benefits: Free 14-week online peer support specialist training. Certificate.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to all. No college enrollment required.
opportunities.process: Register online at CCSF website.
opportunities.notes: Free. Online. No prerequisites. Leads to certification.

opportunities.benefits: 24/7 mental health and substance use crisis services. Entry point to all SF behavioral health services.
opportunities.eligibility: All San Francisco residents. Anyone in crisis.
opportunities.process: Walk in or call 24/7.
opportunities.notes: Gateway to all SFDPH behavioral health services. Open 24/7.

opportunities.benefits: Free therapy with harm reduction approach. No sobriety requirement.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone. Especially people who use drugs, people in recovery, sex workers.
opportunities.process: Call or email to schedule intake.
opportunities.notes: Free therapy. No requirement to be sober or want to stop using.

opportunities.benefits: Free residential substance use treatment. Culturally-based (Native American) but open to all.
opportunities.eligibility: Adults seeking residential treatment. All welcome.
opportunities.process: Call for intake. Bed available based on openings.
opportunities.notes: Free residential treatment. Cultural programming.

opportunities.benefits: Free year-long residential substance use treatment. Transitional housing.
opportunities.eligibility: Men seeking long-term residential treatment.
opportunities.process: Call for intake. Available to walk-ins.
opportunities.address: 121 Golden Gate Ave, San Francisco, CA 94102
opportunities.notes: Free year-long residential program.

opportunities.benefits: Harm reduction counseling, contingency management (gift cards), group support for gay/bi men who use stimulants.
opportunities.eligibility: Gay and bisexual men who use meth/cocaine. SF residents.
opportunities.process: Call or walk in to Strut (470 Castro St).
checklist: Call or walk in to Strut
opportunities.notes: Culturally-specific program. Gift card incentives.

opportunities.benefits: Peer support groups, harm reduction, Narcan training, mental health counseling, case management.
opportunities.eligibility: Low-income individuals, people in recovery, Tenderloin residents.
opportunities.process: Walk in or call. Most services same-day.
opportunities.notes: Walk-in friendly. Same-day services. Tenderloin location.

opportunities.benefits: Mental health services, substance use treatment, street outreach, residential care.
opportunities.eligibility: Low-income individuals, people with mental illness, people experiencing homelessness.
opportunities.process: Call or walk in to access services.
opportunities.notes: Multiple locations. Wide range of services.

opportunities.benefits: SSI/SSDI advocacy, employment services, mental health support, vocational training. Stipends available.

opportunities.notes: Stipends and transportation assistance available for employment program participants.

opportunities.benefits: 24/7 drug sobering center. Safe place to come down. Medical monitoring. No police.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone intoxicated on drugs who needs a safe place. No requirements.

opportunities.notes: No police. No judgment. Just a safe place to sober up.

opportunities.source: Harm Reduction Therapy Center Free Therapy
opportunities.benefits: Free individual and group therapy. Harm reduction approach. No sobriety required.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone seeking therapy. No insurance required.
opportunities.process: Call or email to schedule.
opportunities.notes: Free therapy. No insurance needed. Harm reduction model.

opportunities.benefits: Free laptop upon completing digital literacy workshops. IT support training, coding basics, hardware repair, apprenticeships, mentors, internships.
opportunities.eligibility: Low-income residents, young adults 16-24, people with employment gaps. No degree required.
opportunities.process: Apply online or attend info session. Complete workshop series. Receive laptop upon graduation.
opportunities.notes: One of the strongest laptop + training pathways in SF. Pre-apprenticeship cohorts available.

opportunities.benefits: Free internet in affordable housing, free Wi-Fi in public spaces, reserve computers at libraries, low-cost devices, digital skills help.
opportunities.eligibility: Low-income residents, seniors, people with disabilities, affordable housing residents.
opportunities.process: Apply through partner programs: Fiber to Housing, Digital Connections, Tech Exchange, Human-I-T, Dev/Mission, or library.
opportunities.notes: Umbrella program coordinating multiple partners. Expected to end 2026.

opportunities.benefits: Free in-unit high-speed internet (60-120 Mbps) for residents of 210+ participating affordable housing properties (20,000+ units).
opportunities.eligibility: Residents of participating affordable housing properties.
opportunities.process: Check with your property manager. If building participates, internet is automatically available.
checklist: Ask property manager about Fiber to Housing
opportunities.notes: Free high-speed internet. No cost to resident.

opportunities.benefits: Free refurbished laptop after completing 8-hour digital skills course. Low-cost devices for families.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to all Bay Area residents. Priority for low-income families with students.
opportunities.process: Apply online. Complete 8-hour digital skills course. Receive laptop.
opportunities.notes: Free laptop after completing a single course. Oakland location but serves Bay Area.

opportunities.source: Goodwill SF Digital Skills + Free Laptop
opportunities.benefits: Free laptop after completing 5-day digital skills class. Job training, career coaching.

opportunities.notes: 5-day class = free laptop. Justice-involved individuals welcome.

opportunities.benefits: Free computer access (40+ computers), job-skill building, digital support, typing practice, resume help, internet. Serves 100+ visitors/day.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone age 18+. Designed for homeless and low-income adults. No residency check.

opportunities.address: 150 Golden Gate Ave, San Francisco, CA 94102
opportunities.notes: Lowest barrier tech access in SF. No requirements. Just show up.

opportunities.benefits: Free computer training for seniors (60+) and adults with disabilities. 50+ locations citywide. Multilingual.

opportunities.benefits: Low-cost home internet (50 Mbps) for $9.95/month. Option to buy low-cost laptop ($149.99). Free for some through ACP.

opportunities.benefits: Find low-cost internet plans ($10-25/month) and discounted computers. Database of offers.

opportunities.benefits: Low-cost refurbished computers, laptops, tablets. Digital literacy classes.
